# Superflow

> A Claude Code skill that orchestrates a full dev workflow: onboarding, product discovery with expert panel brainstorming, autonomous execution with adaptive governance and PR-per-sprint, and merge. Hybrid project — markdown prompts + Python companion CLI (supervisor).

## Overview

Superflow is a 4-phase development workflow skill for Claude Code:

- **Phase 0 (Onboarding):** Auto-detects project state and confirms with user, analyzes codebase with 5 parallel agents (4 Claude + 1 Codex), generates health report, creates/audits llms.txt and CLAUDE.md, sets up permissions — structured as 5 modular stage files
- **Phase 1 (Product Discovery):** Expert panel brainstorming (3-4 parallel persona agents producing a Board Memo), governance mode selection (light/standard/critical), dual-model spec/plan review, Autonomy Charter generation, auto-launch flow
- **Phase 2 (Autonomous Execution):** Sprint-based autonomous development — git worktrees, TDD, Popen-based execution with intra-sprint progress polling, governance-aware review tiering, charter compliance checks, blocker escalation, one PR per sprint
- **Phase 3 (Merge):** User-initiated sequential rebase merge with CI verification, structured completion data (`completion-data.json`), and documentation update

Version: v4.0.0 (2026-03-26). MIT License.

## File Structure

- [SKILL.md](SKILL.md): Entry point — startup checklist, provider detection, supervisor detection, phase routing (~142 lines)
- [superflow-enforcement.md](superflow-enforcement.md): 9 hard rules that survive context compaction — deployed to ~/.claude/rules/ (80 lines)
- [CLAUDE.md](CLAUDE.md): Project instructions for Claude — architecture, key files table, conventions (~130 lines)
- [README.md](README.md): Installation, usage, supervisor docs, v4.0 features, permissions setup

## Phase References

- [references/phase0-onboarding.md](references/phase0-onboarding.md): Phase 0 router — detection, recovery matrix, stage loading (~80 lines)
- [references/phase0/stage1-detect.md](references/phase0/stage1-detect.md): Parallel preflight, auto-detection, confirmation (~214 lines)
- [references/phase0/stage2-analysis.md](references/phase0/stage2-analysis.md): 5 parallel agents, tiered model usage (~256 lines)
- [references/phase0/stage3-report.md](references/phase0/stage3-report.md): Health report, informative summary, approval (~179 lines)
- [references/phase0/stage4-setup.md](references/phase0/stage4-setup.md): 3 concurrent branches, strict file ownership (~238 lines)
- [references/phase0/stage5-completion.md](references/phase0/stage5-completion.md): Markers, tech debt persistence, restart (~163 lines)
- [references/phase0/greenfield.md](references/phase0/greenfield.md): Greenfield path G1-G6 (~350 lines)
- [references/phase1-discovery.md](references/phase1-discovery.md): Product discovery — expert panel brainstorming (Board Memo pattern), governance mode selection, spec/plan review, Autonomy Charter generation, auto-launch (~374 lines)
- [references/phase2-execution.md](references/phase2-execution.md): Autonomous sprint execution — Popen with progress polling, governance-aware review tiering, charter compliance, blocker escalation, holistic review (~350 lines)
- [references/phase3-merge.md](references/phase3-merge.md): Merge workflow — pre-merge checklist, sequential rebase, CI handling, structured completion-data.json, post-merge report (184 lines)

## Agent Prompts

- [prompts/implementer.md](prompts/implementer.md): TDD-driven code implementation agent — Red-Green-Refactor cycle, test evidence required (81 lines)
- [prompts/expert-panel.md](prompts/expert-panel.md): Expert persona prompt for brainstorming — proposals, challenge section, priority recommendation (44 lines)
- [prompts/spec-reviewer.md](prompts/spec-reviewer.md): Spec compliance reviewer — verifies implementation matches specification (55 lines)
- [prompts/code-quality-reviewer.md](prompts/code-quality-reviewer.md): Code quality reviewer — correctness, security, charter compliance, OWASP (82 lines)
- [prompts/product-reviewer.md](prompts/product-reviewer.md): Product acceptance reviewer — user perspective, charter compliance, edge cases (76 lines)
- [prompts/llms-txt-writer.md](prompts/llms-txt-writer.md): llms.txt generation/audit prompt — llmstxt.org standard, verified entries only (154 lines)
- [prompts/claude-md-writer.md](prompts/claude-md-writer.md): CLAUDE.md generation/audit prompt — verified paths and commands (148 lines)
- [prompts/testing-guidelines.md](prompts/testing-guidelines.md): Testing reference — TDD cycle, coverage expectations, included in implementer context (68 lines)
- [prompts/codex/code-reviewer.md](prompts/codex/code-reviewer.md): Codex code quality review prompt
- [prompts/codex/product-reviewer.md](prompts/codex/product-reviewer.md): Codex product acceptance review prompt
- [prompts/codex/audit.md](prompts/codex/audit.md): Codex onboarding audit prompt

## Agent Definitions

12 agent definitions in `agents/` with reasoning tier frontmatter (deep/standard/fast):
- Deep tier (Opus, high effort): deep-implementer, deep-code-reviewer, deep-product-reviewer, deep-spec-reviewer, deep-doc-writer, deep-analyst
- Standard tier (Sonnet, medium effort): standard-implementer, standard-code-reviewer, standard-product-reviewer, standard-spec-reviewer, standard-doc-writer
- Fast tier (Sonnet, low effort): fast-implementer

## Supervisor System

The supervisor is a Python CLI that orchestrates autonomous multi-sprint execution outside of Claude Code's context window.

- [bin/superflow-supervisor](bin/superflow-supervisor): CLI entry point — argparse-based with run/status/resume/reset/launch/stop commands (213 lines)
- [lib/supervisor.py](lib/supervisor.py): Core supervisor — Popen-based execution with progress polling, charter injection, governance-aware review tiering, digest, blocker escalation, completion data (~1970 lines, ~1580 LOC)
- [lib/queue.py](lib/queue.py): Sprint queue — JSON-based queue with DAG dependency resolution, metadata dict, atomic saves (137 lines, ~120 LOC)
- [lib/planner.py](lib/planner.py): Plan-to-queue + charter-to-queue generator, shared heading parser, queue freshness validation (~336 lines, ~280 LOC)
- [lib/checkpoint.py](lib/checkpoint.py): Checkpoint system — save/load sprint execution state for crash recovery with atomic writes, string IDs, named checkpoints (52 lines, ~44 LOC)
- [lib/parallel.py](lib/parallel.py): Parallel execution — ThreadPoolExecutor-based concurrent sprint execution with queue locking (61 lines, ~50 LOC)
- [lib/replanner.py](lib/replanner.py): Adaptive replanner — analyzes completed sprints, charter-aware, adjusts remaining plans via Claude subprocess (225 lines, ~180 LOC)
- [lib/notifications.py](lib/notifications.py): Notifications — Telegram Bot API integration with stdout fallback, 20 event types including progress, digest, blocker escalation, merge reminder (196 lines, ~160 LOC)
- [templates/supervisor-sprint-prompt.md](templates/supervisor-sprint-prompt.md): Sprint execution prompt template with charter, governance, progress placeholders (69 lines)
- [templates/replan-prompt.md](templates/replan-prompt.md): Replanner prompt template with charter placeholder (29 lines)
- [examples/sprint-queue-example.json](examples/sprint-queue-example.json): Queue file template with metadata example (56 lines)

## Tests

362+ tests across 11 test files (~7100 lines total):
- [tests/test_supervisor.py](tests/test_supervisor.py): Core supervisor tests — charter injection, Popen execution, governance tiering, digest, blocker escalation (3643 lines)
- [tests/test_integration.py](tests/test_integration.py): Integration tests — happy path, crash recovery, charter flow, governance modes (610 lines)
- [tests/test_queue.py](tests/test_queue.py): Queue tests — DAG resolution, metadata, atomic saves, status transitions (568 lines)
- [tests/test_notifications.py](tests/test_notifications.py): Notification tests — 20 event types, progress, digest, blocker, merge reminder (376 lines)
- [tests/test_replanner.py](tests/test_replanner.py): Replanner tests — JSON parsing, charter context, sprint modification (336 lines)
- [tests/test_planner.py](tests/test_planner.py): Planner tests — plan-to-queue, charter-to-queue, heading parser (443 lines)
- [tests/test_launcher.py](tests/test_launcher.py): Launcher tests — launch/stop/status/restart, PID management (452 lines)
- [tests/test_parallel.py](tests/test_parallel.py): Parallel execution tests — concurrency, queue locking, dependency handling (219 lines)
- [tests/test_cli.py](tests/test_cli.py): CLI argument parsing and command dispatch tests (319 lines)
- [tests/test_checkpoint.py](tests/test_checkpoint.py): Checkpoint save/load and atomic write tests (147 lines)

## Key Concepts

- **Autonomy Charter:** Durable intent artifact generated at end of Phase 1. Contains goal, non-negotiables, success criteria, and governance mode. Injected into every sprint prompt, reviewer context, and replanner — single source of truth for autonomous execution boundaries
- **Adaptive governance:** Three modes (light/standard/critical) auto-suggested based on task dimensions. Controls review depth (`_required_par_keys()`), holistic review threshold (`_should_run_holistic()`), and plan structure. Light mode uses `charter_to_queue()` for inline sprint plans
- **Expert panel brainstorming:** Phase 1 Steps 4-6 dispatch 3-4 parallel expert persona agents (Product GM, Staff Engineer, UX/Workflow, Domain Expert). Outputs synthesized into Board Memo with consensus, disagreements, risks, and decisions needed
- **2-agent specialized review:** Every PR is reviewed by 2 agents in parallel — Claude (Product lens: spec fit, user scenarios, charter compliance) + Codex/secondary (Technical lens: correctness, security, charter compliance). Governance mode controls whether single or dual review
- **Review gate:** Mandatory gate before every PR push — all reviewers must return ACCEPTED, evidence written to `.par-evidence.json`
- **Final Holistic Review:** After all sprints complete (conditional on governance mode and sprint count), two Opus reviewers review ALL code together to catch cross-module issues
- **Popen execution with polling:** `subprocess.Popen` replaces `subprocess.run` for sprint execution, enabling live heartbeat writes and intra-sprint progress tracking via `.superflow/sprint-progress.json`
- **Progress digest:** Periodic summary notifications sent every N completed sprints (configurable via `digest_interval`), including PR URLs and next sprint info
- **Blocker escalation:** When a sprint exhausts retries, `notify_blocker_escalation()` sends a prominent notification instead of a regular failure message
- **Completion data:** `_write_completion_data()` writes structured `completion-data.json` with sprint results, PR URLs, and metadata for Phase 3 context
- **Breakage Scenario:** Every review finding must include a concrete scenario where the issue causes a real problem — no scenario = not a finding
- **Compaction resilience:** Enforcement rules live in `~/.claude/rules/` (auto-loaded by Claude Code), phase docs are re-read from disk at boundaries
- **Markers:** `<!-- updated-by-superflow:YYYY-MM-DD -->` in generated files — used to detect whether Phase 0 has run
- **Sprint queue:** JSON file defining sprints with DAG dependencies and metadata dict (charter_file, governance_mode), processed by the supervisor CLI
- **Checkpoints:** Per-sprint JSON files storing execution state, enabling crash recovery via `resume` command

<!-- updated-by-superflow:2026-03-26 -->
